name: Sicilian Chicken Thighs
description: Bold and savory chicken thighs braised in a rich tomato sauce with olives, capers, and aromatic herbs — a classic Sicilian-style dish bursting with Mediterranean flavor.
instructions: Pat chicken thighs dry with paper towels and season generously with salt and black pepper on both sides.
Heat olive oil in a large oven-safe skillet or Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Place chicken thighs skin-side down and sear for 5–6 minutes until golden and crispy. Flip and sear the other side for 3 minutes. Remove and set aside.
In the same pan, reduce heat to medium. Add onion and cook for 3–4 minutes until softened. Add garlic and red pepper flakes and cook for 1 minute.
Pour in white wine and scrape up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan. Let simmer for 2 minutes.
Add crushed tomatoes, chicken broth, olives, capers, and oregano. Stir to combine.
Nestle the chicken thighs back into the pan skin-side up. Bring to a simmer.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 25–30 minutes until the chicken is cooked through (internal temp 165°F).
Uncover for the last 5 minutes to allow the sauce to thicken slightly.
Garnish with fresh parsley and serve with crusty bread, pasta, or rice.
notes: For extra depth, add a pinch of cinnamon and a handful of raisins — a true Sicilian touch. Leftovers taste even better the next day.

name: Grilled Moroccan Chicken
description: Tender grilled chicken marinated in a vibrant blend of Moroccan spices including cumin, coriander, paprika, and turmeric, finished with a squeeze of lemon and fresh herbs.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, cumin, coriander, smoked paprika, turmeric, cinnamon, cayenne, salt, and pepper.
Add chicken thighs to the marinade and toss to coat thoroughly. Cover and refrigerate for at least 1 hour, or up to 8 hours.
Preheat grill or grill pan to medium-high heat. Oil the grates lightly.
Remove chicken from marinade, letting excess drip off.
Grill chicken for 5–6 minutes per side until nicely charred and internal temperature reaches 165°F.
Remove from grill and let rest for 5 minutes.
Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve with lemon wedges, couscous, or flatbread.
notes: This marinade also works beautifully with bone-in chicken pieces — just increase the grill time. Serve with a simple cucumber-tomato salad and tzatziki for a full meal.

name: Chicken Satay
description: Juicy skewered chicken marinated in a fragrant blend of lemongrass, coconut milk, and spices, grilled to perfection and served with a rich and creamy peanut dipping sauce.
instructions: In a bowl, mix together coconut milk, soy sauce, fish sauce, brown sugar, turmeric, cumin, coriander, and garlic to make the marinade.
Add chicken strips to the marinade and toss to coat. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes or up to 4 hours.

Meanwhile, make the peanut sauce: Combine peanut butter, coconut milk, soy sauce, lime juice, brown sugar, and sriracha in a small saucepan. Whisk over low heat until smooth and warmed through. Set aside.
Thread marinated chicken strips onto soaked wooden skewers.
Preheat grill or grill pan to medium-high heat and lightly oil the surface.
Grill skewers for 3–4 minutes per side until cooked through and slightly charred.
Serve immediately with peanut dipping sauce and garnish with sliced cucumber and fresh lime wedges.
notes: If you don't have a grill, these cook well in the oven at 425°F for 12–15 minutes. Thin the peanut sauce with a splash of warm water if it becomes too thick.

name: Lemon Chicken Soup
description: A bright, comforting chicken soup made with tender shredded chicken, fresh vegetables, and a generous squeeze of lemon for a refreshing, clean flavor.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add onion and cook for 4–5 minutes until softened.
Add garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add carrots and celery and stir to combine. Cook for 3 minutes.
Pour in chicken broth and add whole chicken breasts, thyme, bay leaves, salt, and pepper.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer for 20–25 minutes until chicken is cooked through.
Remove chicken breasts and shred with two forks.
Discard bay leaves. Return shredded chicken to the pot.
Stir in lemon juice and lemon zest. Taste and adjust seasoning.
Stir in fresh parsley and serve hot.
notes: For a heartier soup, add 1 cup of small pasta or white rice in the last 10 minutes of cooking.

name: Lemon Chicken Orzo Soup
description: A Greek-inspired chicken soup loaded with tender orzo pasta, shredded chicken, fresh lemon, and herbs — hearty, bright, and deeply satisfying.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add onion and sauté for 4–5 minutes until translucent.
Add garlic, carrots, and celery. Cook for 3–4 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Pour in chicken broth and add whole chicken breasts, bay leaf, oregano, salt, and pepper.
Bring to a boil, then reduce to a simmer. Cook for 20 minutes until chicken is cooked through.
Remove chicken and shred with two forks. Discard bay leaf.
Bring the broth back to a gentle boil. Add orzo and cook for 8–10 minutes until tender.
Return shredded chicken to the pot. Stir in baby spinach and let wilt for 1–2 minutes.
Add lemon juice, lemon zest, and fresh dill. Stir and taste for seasoning.
Serve hot with crusty bread.
notes: Orzo continues to absorb liquid as it sits, so add extra broth when reheating leftovers. Substitute kale for spinach if preferred.

name: Turmeric Chicken Soup
description: A nourishing, anti-inflammatory chicken soup infused with golden turmeric, ginger, and garlic — warming, wholesome, and packed with healing spices.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add onion and sauté for 4–5 minutes until softened.
Add garlic and fresh ginger. Cook for 1–2 minutes until fragrant.
Stir in turmeric, cumin, and black pepper. Cook the spices with the aromatics for 1 minute.
Add chicken thighs, carrots, and chicken broth. Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 25 minutes until chicken is cooked through.
Remove chicken and shred with two forks. Return to the pot.
Stir in coconut milk and bring back to a gentle simmer.
Add baby spinach and cook for 1–2 minutes until wilted.
Stir in lemon juice. Taste and adjust salt and pepper.
Ladle into bowls and garnish with fresh parsley. Serve with crusty bread or rice.
notes: The black pepper helps activate the curcumin in turmeric, boosting its anti-inflammatory benefits. Add a pinch of cayenne if you like more heat.

name: Lemon Pecorino Chicken
description: Pan-seared chicken cutlets coated in crispy Pecorino Romano and finished with a bright, buttery lemon pan sauce — elegant yet simple enough for any weeknight.
instructions: Set up a breading station with three shallow bowls: flour in the first, beaten eggs in the second, and a mixture of breadcrumbs and grated Pecorino Romano in the third.
Season chicken breasts with salt and pepper.
Dredge each piece in flour, dip in egg, then press firmly into the Pecorino breadcrumb mixture to coat both sides.
Heat olive oil and 1 tablespoon of butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add chicken and cook for 4–5 minutes per side until golden brown and cooked through. Work in batches if needed. Transfer to a plate.
In the same skillet, reduce heat to medium and add remaining butter. Add garlic and cook for 30 seconds.
Pour in chicken broth and lemon juice. Scrape up any browned bits and simmer for 2–3 minutes until slightly reduced.
Stir in lemon zest and taste for seasoning.
Return chicken to the pan and spoon sauce over the top. Garnish with fresh parsley and serve immediately.
notes: Pecorino Romano is saltier than Parmesan, so go easy on added salt. This dish pairs beautifully with arugula salad or roasted asparagus.

name: Chicken Breakfast Sausage
description: Homemade lean chicken breakfast sausage patties seasoned with sage, thyme, and a hint of maple syrup — a healthier and delicious alternative to traditional pork sausage.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine ground chicken, maple syrup, sage, thyme, garlic powder, onion powder, smoked paprika, red pepper flakes, salt, and black pepper.
Mix gently until all seasonings are evenly incorporated. Do not overmix.
Divide the mixture into 8 equal portions and shape each into a patty about 1/2 inch thick.
Heat olive oil in a large non-stick skillet over medium heat.
Add patties in a single layer (work in batches if needed) and cook for 4–5 minutes per side until golden brown and cooked through (internal temp 165°F).
Serve immediately with eggs and toast, or store in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 4 days.
notes: These patties freeze beautifully — lay them flat on a parchment-lined baking sheet to freeze, then transfer to a zip-top bag. Reheat in a skillet or microwave.

name: Chicken Asparagus Stir Fry
description: A quick and colorful stir fry featuring tender chicken strips and crisp asparagus tossed in a savory garlic-ginger sauce — ready in under 30 minutes.

ingredients.name: asparagus
ingredients.prepNote: tough ends trimmed, cut into 2-inch pieces

instructions: In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, oyster sauce, sesame oil, chicken broth, cornstarch, and sugar to make the sauce. Set aside.
Toss sliced chicken with 1 tablespoon of the sauce mixture and a pinch of cornstarch. Let marinate for 10 minutes.
Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large wok or skillet over high heat until smoking.
Add chicken in a single layer and cook without stirring for 2 minutes. Stir and cook for another 2–3 minutes until cooked through. Remove and set aside.
Add remaining oil to the pan. Add asparagus and bell pepper and stir fry for 3–4 minutes until crisp-tender.
Add garlic, ginger, and red pepper flakes. Stir fry for 30 seconds.
Return chicken to the pan and pour in the sauce. Toss everything together and cook for 1–2 minutes until the sauce thickens and coats everything.
Transfer to a serving dish and garnish with sesame seeds and green onions.
Serve immediately over steamed rice or noodles.
notes: High heat is key for good stir fry — do not overcrowd the pan. Work in batches if necessary to keep the temperature up.

name: Pad Thai (with Chicken)
description: A classic Thai street-food noodle dish with chewy rice noodles, tender chicken, scrambled egg, bean sprouts, and a tangy tamarind-based sauce, topped with crushed peanuts and lime.

ingredients.name: flat rice noodles
ingredients.prepNote: soaked in warm water for 20 minutes until pliable

instructions: In a small bowl, whisk together tamarind paste, fish sauce, soy sauce, brown sugar, and sriracha to make the Pad Thai sauce. Set aside.
Drain soaked rice noodles and set aside.
Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a large wok over high heat. Add chicken and cook for 3–4 minutes until fully cooked. Push to the side.
Add another tablespoon of oil. Add garlic (and dried shrimp if using) and stir fry for 30 seconds.
Add drained noodles to the wok and pour the Pad Thai sauce over them. Toss well to combine. If noodles stick, add a splash of water.
Push everything to the side of the wok. Add remaining oil and crack in the eggs. Scramble lightly and then fold into the noodles before fully set.
Add bean sprouts and green onions. Toss everything together and cook for 1–2 minutes.
Divide onto plates. Top with crushed peanuts, fresh cilantro, and lime wedges. Serve immediately.
notes: The key to authentic Pad Thai is high heat. Do not overcrowd the wok. If you don't have tamarind paste, a mix of lime juice and a bit of extra brown sugar can work as a substitute.

name: Instant Pot Chicken Noodle Soup
description: The ultimate classic chicken noodle soup made fast and easy in the Instant Pot — rich, golden broth with tender chicken, egg noodles, and fresh vegetables, ready in about 40 minutes.
instructions: Set the Instant Pot to Sauté mode. Add olive oil and heat until shimmering.
Add onion and sauté for 3 minutes until softened. Add garlic and cook for 30 seconds.
Add carrots and celery and stir briefly.
Add chicken pieces, chicken broth, water, thyme, dried parsley, bay leaves, salt, and pepper.
Lock the lid and set the valve to Sealing. Cook on Manual (High Pressure) for 15 minutes.
When the timer goes off, allow the pressure to naturally release for 10 minutes, then quick release any remaining pressure.
Carefully open the lid. Remove chicken pieces and shred the meat, discarding bones and skin. Discard bay leaves.
Set the Instant Pot back to Sauté mode. Add egg noodles and cook for 6–8 minutes until tender.
Return shredded chicken to the pot. Taste and adjust seasoning.
Ladle into bowls and garnish with fresh parsley. Serve hot.
notes: If you want to add the noodles before pressure cooking, they may become too soft. Cook them after on Sauté mode for the best texture. Leftovers can be stored for up to 4 days — add more broth when reheating as noodles absorb liquid.
